body:
**Scope:** the **rebalance custodian** + the **declarative reconciliation hook** + **capacity telemetry**. Proactively move fragments off **draining / decommissioning** D servers, **preserving the failure-domain invariant** (§6.3 step 3) — each move the same commit-point-atomic re-place as a reconstruction (where rebalance and durability spread conflict, **spread wins**). Driven by operator **desired state** reconciled by the custodian (§8.4): build the desired-state read/write + reconciliation-status **hook** (drain/decommission), single-zone, so *"policy changed"* and *"policy satisfied"* are distinct observable moments. Emit **per-failure-domain utilization** (capacity plane). Hot-spot rebalance is the lighter, measurement-driven part and may trail drain/decommission.

**Definition of done**
- [ ] a drained/decommissioned D server is evacuated, preserving the failure-domain invariant
- [ ] desired-state (drain) is reconciled; "changed" vs "satisfied" are observable
- [ ] per-failure-domain utilization is emitted
- [ ] each move is commit-point-atomic (crash leaves garbage, not corruption)

**Needs:** M3.6. **Refs:** proposal 0005 §rebalance/§"Declarative management hook"; architecture §6.3, §8.4, §8.3; ADR-0011/0013.

milestone.title: M3 — Custodians
milestone.description: The self-maintaining durability plane (L4): GC, scrub, reconstruction, rebalance + durability telemetry. Makes single-zone trustworthy; gates the Step-2 release. Plan: proposal 0005.

title: M3.7 — Rebalance + declarative drain/decommission + capacity telemetry
